meaning: adj.无法忍受的,不能忍耐的,不能持久的
examples: Isaac had found the work unendurable and walked out of the job.
His impatience to get to London grew hourly, and his captivity became almost unendurable.
What is it that I especially find utterly unendurable?
Rumors and wisecracks help men endure the unendurable, which is why they are integral to every war.
But colleagues were grateful for his vivid wit, because without it his brilliance would have been unendurable.
